content:
{
    "improvements": [
        {
            "id": "IMP-001",
            "description": "Define explicit owner names/roles per sprint and per task, not just 'maintainer' for every row in the risk register and Phase 0 table",
            "rationale": "Single-owner 'maintainer' across 25+ rows creates a bus-factor of 1 and obscures accountability. For a 12-week, 5-sprint cycle with concurrent dependencies (e.g., T4.6 vendor concurrency tuning vs T5.2 smoke-fleet workflow), distinct DRIs prevent serialization bottlenecks and enable parallel execution. Without named owners, the per-sprint ship/no-ship gate has no clear escalation path.",
            "location": "Phase 0 table, every Sprint Risks table, Cycle-Wide Risk Register",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-002",
            "description": "Add explicit wall-clock budget and effort estimates (engineer-days) per sprint, beyond the '2-week ceiling'",
            "rationale": "The plan declares a 12-week hard ceiling and 2-week-per-sprint scope-cut trigger but provides no per-task or per-sprint effort estimate. Sprint 4 has 10 tasks including vendor-side fixes (#774 cheval long-prompt) that may require upstream coordination — this cannot fit a 2-week box without estimation. Without estimates, the 'scope-cut at sprint review' gate fires reactively instead of being planned.",
            "location": "Each Sprint header (Scope: LARGE/MEDIUM is qualitative only)",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.9
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-003",
            "description": "Specify rollback procedure for the audit envelope schema bump (1.1.0 → 1.2.0) when downstream consumers are mid-flight",
            "rationale": "T1.2 declares the bump 'additive only' with rollback via 'additive removal,' but does not address what happens to in-flight audit records emitted under 1.2.0 if the schema is rolled back. Cross-runtime byte-equality gate runs before merge, but there is no defined procedure for emergency rollback after merge if a consumer (L1-L7 emitter) breaks. This is a Sprint 1 dependency that gates all subsequent sprints.",
            "location": "Sprint 1, T1.2 + S1.R1 mitigation",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.8
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-004",
            "description": "Define concrete pass/fail thresholds for T4.6 parallel-dispatch concurrency baseline (currently '≥5/6 succeed' is single-point)",
            "rationale": "AC-4.5c specifies '≥5/6 succeed' on a 12-30K prompt fixture, but this is a single sample. Vendor connection-pool behavior is non-deterministic — a single 5/6 run could pass while p50 is actually 3/6. Need: N=20 runs, p50/p95/p99 success rates, with regression threshold (e.g., >50% degradation between runs triggers sequential-fallback).",
            "location": "Sprint 4, T4.6 + AC-4.5c.test",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-005",
            "description": "Add explicit handling for the case where Phase 0 P0-1 (beads migration) cannot be resolved",
            "rationale": "P0-1 is BLOCKING but the fallback is 'declare 24h opt-out + manual NOTES.md tracking' — this is open-ended and conflicts with the CLAUDE.md NEVER rule. Need a hard deadline (e.g., 'if P0-1 is not resolved within 48h of cycle kickoff, escalate to upstream beads_rust 0.2.1 maintainers AND file blocking issue on cycle-102'). Otherwise the cycle could start with indefinite manual tracking.",
            "location": "Phase 0, P0-1 + fallback paragraph",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.75
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-006",
            "description": "Decouple T5.E2E M1 baseline measurement from cycle-ship gate to avoid circular dependency",
            "rationale": "M1 is a 30-day post-ship invariant, but T5.E2E lists M1 as one of 8 goals to validate with pass condition 'Count = 0 (or trip → file hotfix-cycle, NOT block cycle-102 ship)'. This is contradictory — if M1 is checked at ship and trips, the conditional says don't block, but it's listed in T5.E2E as a P0 gate. Either remove M1 from T5.E2E pass criteria OR explicitly mark it as 'baseline-only, advisory.' Currently the audit-sprint sentence says any failure blocks ship.",
            "location": "Sprint 5, T5.E2E table M1 row + 'E2E artifact contents' paragraph",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.8
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-007",
            "description": "Add abort/recovery procedure for partial sprint completion across the linear dependency chain",
            "rationale": "The plan states 'Sprint order = dependency order (linear chain)' which means a Sprint 2 failure blocks Sprints 3-5 entirely. There is no defined procedure for partial completion (e.g., if T2.9 Bedrock validation fails but T2.1-T2.8 pass, can Sprint 3 proceed without Bedrock support?). Need explicit minimum-viable-pass criteria per sprint that allows downstream sprints to start with documented gaps.",
            "location": "Executive Summary + per-sprint Definition of Done",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.75
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-008",
            "description": "Specify smoke-fleet cost monitoring and alerting separately from per-run budget cap",
            "rationale": "S5.R1 estimates $84/wk worst case but provides no daily/weekly cost monitoring mechanism. Need: cumulative cost ledger emitted to audit envelope, alert threshold at 80% of monthly budget, and explicit kill-switch if cumulative cost > 2× expected. Currently only per-run cap exists, which won't catch a slow drift to 24×/day × 30 days = 720 runs/month at slightly elevated cost.",
            "location": "Sprint 5, T5.2 + T5.3 deliverables",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.8
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-009",
            "description": "Clarify the Bridgebuilder review gate: define exact criteria for 'kaironic plateau acceptable'",
            "rationale": "Every sprint DoD says 'Bridgebuilder review on sprint PR (kaironic plateau acceptable per cycle-099 precedent — must include substantive subagent review with ≥1 HIGH finding fixed).' But what counts as 'substantive'? What if BB returns 0 HIGH findings — is that a pass or stall? Need explicit decision tree: (a) full BB success, (b) plateau with N findings, (c) plateau with 0 findings, (d) all 3 BB providers unavailable. Currently (c) is ambiguous.",
            "location": "Sprint 1 DoD (and replicated in Sprints 2-5)",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.75
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-010",
            "description": "Add data-retention/archival policy for fixture replay corpora used in T4.7 (#774) and T4.8 (#757) long-prompt tests",
            "rationale": "Sprint 4 T4.7 and T4.8 capture vendor-side responses (≥50KB prompts to OpenAI, gemini, codex). These fixtures may contain sensitive vendor metadata, rate-limit headers, or even leaked prompt content. The plan does not specify (a) where fixtures are stored, (b) retention period, (c) redaction rules before commit, (d) revocation if a vendor requests removal. NFR-Sec-1 redaction (T1.7) covers logs but not test fixtures.",
            "location": "Sprint 4, T4.7 + T4.8 deliverables",
            "priority": "LOW",
            "confidence": 0.7
        }
    ],
    "summary": "10 improvements identified, 4 HIGH priority, 5 MEDIUM, 1 LOW. Focus areas: ownership/accountability (IMP-001), effort estimation (IMP-002), schema rollback procedures (IMP-003), statistical validation rigor (IMP-004), and dependency-chain failure recovery (IMP-007)."
}
